jokes.text: Due to budget cutbacks, we will no longer be offering free squid guts in the kitchen. (Sobbing)
jokes.setup: Hermes announces budget cutbacks in bureaucratic fashion
jokes.punchline: The 'perk' being cut is disgusting squid guts, plus someone actually sobs about losing it
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama absurdism - treating gross alien food as a valued employee benefit. Good character work from bureaucrat Hermes.

jokes.text: I like how it's not killing us so far.
jokes.setup: Professor shows off his latest invention
jokes.punchline: Fry's low expectations for Professor's inventions - mere survival is noteworthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line - captures his dim optimism and the Professor's track record of dangerous inventions with excellent economy.

jokes.text: You see, as I age, I've been shrinking and feeling colder. So I now need twice as many sweaters, in a smaller size.
jokes.setup: Professor explains why he built a duplicator machine
jokes.punchline: The reason is incredibly specific and mundane - just wants smaller sweaters
jokes.explanation: Great Professor character moment - uses advanced science for petty personal reasons. The specificity sells it.

jokes.text: So that's where baby sweaters come from.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates duplicator making smaller sweaters
jokes.punchline: Fry treats it like learning about biological reproduction
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to 'birds and bees' explanations with perfect Fry innocence. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: That's 'eerie' with two e's.
jokes.setup: Narrator mentions 'the eerie canal'
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily clarifies the spelling of 'eerie'
jokes.explanation: Mild parody of Twilight Zone's portentous narration style. The pedantic spelling clarification is unexpectedly mundane.

jokes.text: Dr. Daniel Zenus, an inventor with a terminal case of 'the lazies.'
jokes.setup: Introducing a scientist character in dramatic fashion
jokes.punchline: His condition is 'the lazies' - treating laziness like a medical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay making laziness sound like a serious medical condition in typical Twilight Zone fashion.

jokes.text: If only I'd programmed the robot to be more careful what I wished for. Robot, experience this tragic irony for me.
jokes.setup: Dr. Zenus realizes his robot has replaced him completely
jokes.punchline: Even experiencing the irony of the situation is too much work - delegates that too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the laziness concept - he's too lazy even for self-reflection. Perfect Twilight Zone parody structure.

jokes.text: Man, I wish we had a robot to do stuff. I know, right?
jokes.setup: Just watched Scary Door episode about robot doing everything
jokes.punchline: Immediately wish for the exact same thing, missing the cautionary tale entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Fry and Leela completely miss the obvious moral of the story. Good ironic structure.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, do you see a robot in this room named 'folder'?
jokes.setup: Professor asks Bender to fold sweaters
jokes.punchline: Bender uses literal interpretation to avoid work - his name isn't 'folder'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - using pedantic logic to avoid work. Perfect character integration and will become a running gag.

jokes.text: Bender, would you mind 'bending' my new sweaters?
jokes.setup: Professor tries to outsmart Bender's literal interpretation
jokes.punchline: Uses 'bending' since that's Bender's name/function
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to Bender's excuse. Professor shows rare cleverness in trying to outmaneuver him.

jokes.text: You want me to do two things? Man, I'd call my lawyer if dialing a phone wasn't such a hassle.
jokes.setup: Bender realizes he has to fold two sweaters
jokes.punchline: Even calling a lawyer to complain is too much work
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Bender's laziness - too lazy even to pursue legal recourse for his laziness. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Razza frazza two things. Ooh. Razza frazza duplicator.
jokes.setup: Bender grumbles about having to do two things
jokes.punchline: Immediately sees the duplicator and gets an idea to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's problem-solving only kicks in when it serves his laziness. Sets up the main plot.

jokes.text: Howdy, fellas. I'm Bender. Go to hell, old man!
jokes.setup: New mini-Bender introduces himself politely
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells original Bender to go to hell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character - even his copies are rude to him. Great contrast between polite greeting and immediate insult.

jokes.text: I like your attitude. Let's party.
jokes.setup: Mini-Bender just told him to go to hell
jokes.punchline: Bender approves of being insulted - recognizes his own personality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Bender appreciates his own awful personality even when it's directed at him.

jokes.text: Both: I'm sorry, do you see a robot in this room named 'folder'?
jokes.setup: Bender asks them to fold sweaters
jokes.punchline: Both mini-Benders use the exact same excuse in unison
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the copies have all of Bender's worst traits. The unison delivery adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Does that mean they only do 60% of the work you do, or that they actually do more work because they're only 60% as lazy?
jokes.setup: Bender explains his copies are 60% scale replicas
jokes.punchline: Leela's logical question about whether laziness scales proportionally
jokes.explanation: Smart observational humor about the mathematics of personality traits. Shows Leela's logical mind.

jokes.text: Aw, and one regular-sized condom. All that and a small wiener? This guy's got it goin' on!
jokes.setup: Reading delivery list for giant alien - huge toiletries and tiny condom
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude interpretation that small condom = small anatomy
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crudeness. Somewhat predictable joke but fits character. The 'got it goin' on' adds personality.

jokes.text: (Leela screams)
jokes.setup: Professor warns them not to react to giant alien's appearance
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately screams upon seeing him
jokes.explanation: Standard setup/payoff - told not to react, immediately reacts. Predictable but economical and functional.

jokes.text: I hope that's vanishing cream, 'cause that needs to go away.
jokes.setup: Giant alien is hideously ugly
jokes.punchline: Leela makes cruel joke about his appearance with product pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun connecting his product (vanishing cream) to his appearance. Cruel but creative wordplay.

jokes.text: Oh, that's cold-blooded!
jokes.setup: Leela makes cruel joke about alien's appearance
jokes.punchline: Mini Bender appreciates the cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender copies appreciate meanness just like the original. Economical reaction beat.

jokes.text: You so ugly, when trick-or-treaters come to your house, they give you candy.
jokes.setup: Classic 'You so ugly' format
jokes.punchline: Reversal - instead of getting candy, people give him candy (out of pity)
jokes.explanation: Solid 'yo mama' style joke with good reversal. Classic format but executed well.

jokes.text: You so ugly, when you go to the bank, they ask you to put on a ski mask!
jokes.setup: Another 'You so ugly' joke
jokes.punchline: Bank asks him to put ON a mask (instead of taking it off like robbers)
jokes.explanation: Another solid reversal joke in the series. Good structure but fairly conventional insult comedy.

jokes.text: You so ugly, you cracked the daily mirror. It's a newspaper!
jokes.setup: You cracked the mirror (from being ugly)
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's the Daily Mirror newspaper, not an actual mirror
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on mirror-breaking joke. The clarification makes it funnier and shows good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Don't feel bad, sir. It's not your fault. You probably just inherited your appearance from your mother.
jokes.setup: Leela starts to console the upset alien kindly
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults his mother instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - seems like she's being nice then accidentally makes it worse. Perfect escalation trigger.

jokes.text: Oh, God, shield your eyes! It's like Edward James Olmos on IMAX!
jokes.setup: Giant alien is attacking and they can see him close up
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference to famously pockmarked actor on huge screen
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture reference that's specific and visual. Good use of IMAX to emphasize the scale/horror.

jokes.text: And that's the story of how one of me became two, and two became four, making seven total. The end.
jokes.setup: Bender explains his duplication to someone
jokes.punchline: His math is completely wrong (1+2+4=7) delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - confidently wrong about simple math. The deadpan 'The end' sells his confidence.

jokes.text: One thing each? Sounds fair. Wait, make that four cigars. Both: You want us to do four things?
jokes.setup: Mini-Benders agree to fetch two cigars (one each)
jokes.punchline: When Bender increases to four cigars, they object to doing 'four things' total instead of two each
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bender's laziness - even when split between multiple robots, they still try to minimize individual effort

jokes.text: Which way to the tiny hookers?
jokes.setup: Tiny Bender appears in the locker room
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for prostitutes scaled to his size
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender priorities - first thing he asks about. Crude but very in-character. Great economy.

jokes.text: It appears to be giving you the finger, sir. Enjoy.
jokes.setup: Tiny Bender is in Hermes' soup
jokes.punchline: Scruffy matter-of-factly describes obscene gesture, then pleasantly says 'Enjoy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy - deadpan observation of crude behavior followed by polite service. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Help me, help me! I'm too lazy to escape!
jokes.setup: Tiny Bender is trapped in soup
jokes.punchline: Could escape but won't because it would require effort
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender character moment - literally too lazy to save himself. Perfect encapsulation of his personality.

jokes.text: It's non-convergent! Oh, dip!
jokes.setup: Professor explains mathematical series is non-convergent
jokes.punchline: Hermes responds with mild exclamation to mathematical crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Professor's technical crisis met with Hermes' bureaucratic understatement.

jokes.text: Wait, does that include me? (Shot fires, Bender groans)
jokes.setup: Professor says they need to destroy all 2,046 Benders
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes he might be included, gets shot mid-question
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing - Bender's concern is immediately 'answered' with violence. Great visual gag.

jokes.text: Too bad he didn't have his own Bible. Just 2,045 more to go.
jokes.setup: Bender appears to be dead
jokes.punchline: Leela makes dark joke about last rites, then matter-of-factly updates the count
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor from usually moral Leela. The casual count update shows how desensitized they are.

jokes.text: Wait, this isn't a real Bender. It's a talking doll from when Bender had that sitcom about the city robot who moves back to the farm. Shut your pumpkin, bumpkin.
jokes.setup: They think they found the last Bender
jokes.punchline: It's merchandise from a ridiculous sitcom premise, complete with corny catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist worldbuilding - Bender had a sitcom with terrible premise. The catchphrase is perfectly corny.

jokes.text: All right, so I let one measly Bender get away. How much harm can one infinitely self-reproducing robot do?
jokes.setup: Leela dismisses the danger of one remaining Bender
jokes.punchline: Describes exactly why it's catastrophically dangerous while dismissing it
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony - character states exactly what will go wrong while dismissing the threat.

jokes.text: Bite my tiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Microscopic Benders appear as grey goo
jokes.punchline: Maintains Bender's signature catchphrase despite being nearly invisible
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback adaptation - modifies the famous catchphrase for the current situation while maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: Like the Kardashians.
jokes.setup: Professor warns about things reproducing without limit, consuming all matter
jokes.punchline: Amy compares to the Kardashians
jokes.explanation: Easy pop culture reference - somewhat cheap but economical and fits Amy's character.

jokes.text: I can no longer face my children!
jokes.setup: All alcohol has disappeared, affecting hardened alcoholics
jokes.punchline: News anchor admits to being a severe alcoholic on live TV
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of alcohol crisis - professional news anchor breaks down on air. Shows societal impact.

jokes.text: I was lying here snoozing, dreaming, oddly enough, about bathing in champagne with six of the world's most distinguished scientists
jokes.setup: Professor describes his dream
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and pretentious dream about bathing with scientists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - his dreams are both hedonistic and pretentiously intellectual.

jokes.text: It's like fine cognac with a hint of aged scrotum.
jokes.setup: Bender tastes the Professor's alcohol-transformed bathwater
jokes.punchline: Disgusting but detailed tasting notes mixing refined and vulgar
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting Bender line. The contrast between 'fine cognac' and 'aged scrotum' is brilliantly gross.

jokes.text: Oh, no! What will I mix with my scotch?
jokes.setup: Professor warns there will be no more fresh water on Earth
jokes.punchline: Bender only cares about his drink mixing options
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - global water crisis = personal drinking problem. Great misdirection from expected concern.

jokes.text: Water is now.... now booze, and everyone's tiddy much protally fit-shaced.
jokes.setup: News anchor trying to report on alcohol crisis
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly drunk on air with slurred speech and spoonerisms
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy through voice acting. The specific spoonerisms are well-crafted drunk speech.

jokes.text: There were no survivors.
jokes.setup: Drunk news anchor reporting on Indy 500
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-factly announces total fatality rate
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economical. The casual delivery of horrific news is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That's your penis. That's my boy.
jokes.setup: Zapp offers to show a picture of his boy
jokes.punchline: It's actually his genitals, which he calls 'my boy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp behavior - crude and inappropriate but delivered with his characteristic confidence.

jokes.text: You're so stinky, you need right guard and left guard.
jokes.setup: Another insult about the alien's hygiene
jokes.punchline: Plays on deodorant brand name to suggest he needs double protection
jokes.explanation: Solid brand name pun. Not groundbreaking but well-constructed and economical.

jokes.text: Your face has been declared a weapon of mass disgusting.
jokes.setup: Another insult about the alien's appearance
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'weapons of mass destruction' with 'disgusting'
jokes.explanation: Good political reference pun. Timely and well-structured, though not highly original.

jokes.text: You mean you'll do two things, and I only have to do one thing? Yeah. Just save the world. Sucker.
jokes.setup: Fry offers to fold both sweaters if Bender saves the world
jokes.punchline: Bender thinks he's getting the better deal - folding sweaters vs saving planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Bender's warped priorities. Perfect irony - world-saving is less important than avoiding menial tasks.

jokes.text: If we all pitch in, we each only have to do one quintillionth of a thing.
jokes.setup: Bender needs to motivate his microscopic copies
jokes.punchline: Reduces the workload to an absurdly small fraction to make it appealing
jokes.explanation: Great math-based humor showing how Bender motivates lazy versions of himself. The precise fraction is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Yeah, if you're into gray dust.
jokes.setup: Giant alien declares 'At last... I'm beautiful!'
jokes.punchline: Bender points out he's just been disintegrated into dust
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender deflation of a dramatic moment. Excellent economy and timing to undercut the alien's final moment.

jokes.text: How, by making me look at you? No. By making you look at... my mama!
jokes.setup: Giant threatens to kill everyone, Bender mocks the method
jokes.punchline: Giant reveals his mother is even uglier than him as the real weapon
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier mama insult with escalation - reveals the insult was prophetic and his mother is the ultimate weapon

jokes.text: think how also we might defeat the monsters of poverty and disease and unliteracy.
jokes.setup: Bender gives an inspiring speech about working together
jokes.punchline: Says 'unliteracy' instead of 'illiteracy' while advocating against it
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay joke - Bender demonstrates illiteracy while speaking against it. Good ironic structure.

jokes.text: Come on, let's go someplace where we don't have to do one quintillionth of a thing all the time.
jokes.setup: Bender tries to inspire continued cooperation
jokes.punchline: Micro Benders reject even the tiny fraction of work he promised
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character consistency - even microscopic fractions of work are too much for Benders.

jokes.text: Maybe I did. Or maybe I rescued one last mini-Bender to fold stupid, ugly sweaters while I laughed at him.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender learned about not being lazy
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals he probably just found another way to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that maintains Bender's character - he learned nothing and found a new way to be lazy and cruel.
